key_drivers: The dollar stayed firm into the U.S. close: TradingView showed DXY at 100.193, up 0.33% on March 27.
Volatility stayed decisively defensive: Cboe listed VIX spot at 31.05, up 13.16%, while CME FedWatch showed 95.9% odds of no change at the April 30, 2026 meeting.
Official U.S. macro rates moved against duration: DFII10 rose to 2.08 from 2.02 and HY OAS widened to 3.21 from 3.17, while GDPNow slowed to 2.0% from 2.33%.
Semiconductor leadership remained weak even as rate volatility eased intraday: SOX fell 1.69% to 7,457.67, while MOVE fell 2.67% to 111.9456.
Several cross-asset inputs remained carried forward from the repository factor snapshot, including oil inventories, gold ETF flows, some central-bank differentials, and China-growth proxies.

stale_sources: Gold ETF flows (carried forward from the repository factor snapshot rather than freshly re-verified in this run)
EIA crude inventories and oil supply classification (carried forward from the prior repository factor snapshot)
China-growth proxy and some FX rate-differential inputs (carried forward from the prior repository factor snapshot)
ECB, RBA, and BoJ stance inputs were reused from the repository factor snapshot rather than fully re-documented from fresh official pages in this run
fallbacks_used: TradingView for DXY, SOX, and MOVE live direction checks
Cboe VIX page for spot volatility level and daily move
Atlanta Fed GDPNow page plus chart extraction for current and prior Q1 growth nowcast values
FRED latest official prints for DFII10, HY OAS, and T10Y2Y
Repository factor snapshot for stale carried-forward oil, ETF-flow, and some FX-specific inputs
overnight_changes: Real yields rose 6 bps day over day, increasing pressure on duration-sensitive assets.
Credit spreads widened while the 2s10s curve steepened, leaving U.S. cyclicals mixed rather than uniformly weak.
The combination of stronger USD and a VIX above 30 kept the broader cross-asset regime defensive through the session.

imminent: Weekend calendar is light; macro focus shifts to Monday event risk and ongoing Iran / Strait of Hormuz headlines
Fed Chair Powell remarks due Monday, March 30, 2026
background: March U.S. payrolls report due next week
Next ECB / RBA / BoJ communication for FX-differential repricing
